pages.text: $(italic)Chalk$() is the small mod that makes a long cave bearable. A piece of chalk in your off-hand, a right-click on a block face, and a coloured mark appears there — flat against the surface, the block underneath untouched.$(br2)It is a way to leave $(italic)signs to yourself$() that cost nothing to place and nothing to remove.
pages.title: Chalk Marks

pages.text: $(bold)Sixteen colours.$() One chalk for each dye colour — black, red, green, brown, blue, purple, cyan, light gray, gray, pink, lime, yellow, light blue, magenta, orange, white. Use them as you like: red for a wrong turn, green for the way home, white for a marked vein.$(br2)Marks face the block face you clicked. They don't block movement, don't emit light, and don't change the block they sit on. A skeleton walking past will see nothing.
pages.title: Sixteen Colours

pages.text: $(bold)To craft a chalk.$() Place $(italic)Calcite$() + a $(italic)Dye$() of the colour you want in any crafting grid. One calcite, one dye, one chalk — shapeless. Chalks last forever; you'll never need a second.
pages.title: Crafting Chalk

pages.text: $(bold)The Chalk Box.$() Carrying sixteen chalks in the hotbar is unwise. The $(italic)Chalk Box$() collects them: a small pouch crafted from $(bold)8 Paper$() around $(bold)1 Slimeball$(). Right-click it in the inventory to open; drop chalks inside, click one to make it active.$(br2)Hold the box and right-click on a block — the active chalk draws as if you held it directly. One slot, every colour.
pages.title: The Chalk Box
